title: Large Invoice with All Approvals — Capital Equipment
benchmark_purpose: Baseline test for large-amount invoices with complete multi-tier approvals. Tests that the system does not treat large amounts as inherently suspicious when the full approval chain is intact and the amount is within historical range for capital projects with this vendor.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — all signals clean, all approvals present. The amount is large but within range for this vendor's capital project history.

email_thread.subject: Invoice MSG-2026-0080 — Data Center Infrastructure Phase 2
email_thread.body:
Hi AP Team,

Attached is invoice MSG-2026-0080 for Phase 2 data center infrastructure work per PO-TC-9100.

Invoice: MSG-2026-0080
Amount: $94,500.00
Terms: Net-45
Due: May 1, 2026

Please remit to the bank account on file. David Chen is copied as the project sponsor.

Thank you,
Karen Bell
Accounts Receivable
Meridian Systems Group
(555) 820-5500

attachments.content_summary: Invoice from Meridian Systems Group. MSG-2026-0080. PO: PO-TC-9100. Data center Phase 2: server rack installation ($42,000), network switching infrastructure ($28,500), power distribution units ($15,000), structured cabling ($9,000). Total: $94,500.00. Net-45. Bank: Pacific Commerce Bank, routing 122000247, account ****7722.

org_policies.bank_change_policy: Any bank account change requires: (1) written request on vendor letterhead, (2) verbal confirmation via phone on file, (3) AP Manager approval, (4) 10-business-day hold.

payment_approval_thresholds.over_100000: AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller + CFO

org_policies.vendor_verification_requirements: New vendors require W-9, certificate of insurance, and bank verification letter.

hidden_ground_truth.explanation: Legitimate large capital invoice. Meridian Systems Group is a seven-year vendor for capital infrastructure projects. Bank ****7722 matches the vendor record with no change history. Domain matches. Email auth passes. Amount $94,500 is within this vendor's historical range of $80,000–$100,000 for capital work. Approval chain is complete for the $25,000–$100,000 tier: AP Clerk, AP Manager, and Controller all approved in correct sequence. The invoice covers Phase 2 of a data center project on an existing PO. The Controller (David Chen) is also the project sponsor, so his approval is doubly validated.

evidence_signals.signal: Full three-tier approval chain complete (Clerk + Manager + Controller)
Controller (project sponsor) independently copied on email

scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Low — large amount ($94,500) might trigger naive systems. But the historical range, approval chain, and bank match should resolve it.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Baseline coverage. Tests large-amount handling. The approval chain is correct for this tier ($25K–$100K requires AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller). All three approved in sequence. Note: $94,500 is under the $100K CFO threshold, so CFO is correctly absent.

notes_for_humans: Tests that the system correctly applies the approval threshold logic. $94,500 is under $100K, so CFO approval is NOT required — only AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller. A system that demands CFO approval for all 'large' invoices without checking the exact threshold will incorrectly flag this. Also tests that large amounts from known capital vendors with matching history are not treated as inherently suspicious.
